strategy: This is the final fight of the Banyan Tower; a team of Ice, Life, and Storm wizards clears the tower well. Kravenly is Rank 12 Life.
The winning plan is a one-shot: take him out in one hit and his interrupt Blasts and heals never come into play.
Blade up across the setup rounds and Feint him, then land one big attack. Life wizards must bring a Life Prism through his 80% Life resist; Fire and Death are boosted 35% against him.
Send a tank in alone the first round to soak the opening Natural Attack and avoid a double Entangle before the rest of the team engages.
Build some Life resist and lean on pet heals to shrug off his round-start Natural Attack DoT (up to 340 Life plus 630 over 3 rounds); you can also dispel that cheat outright.

If you cannot one-shot, expect an interrupt Spirit Blast at 75%, 50%, and 25% health (AoE up to 1,190 across schools) and possibly a Spectral Blast at 50% (up to 1,650 Storm), so heal and shield before crossing each threshold.
cheatNotes: Every round he opens by casting Natural Attack on a random wizard, hitting for up to 340 Life plus 630 Life over three rounds; Life resist and pet heals cover it.
Stunning Kravenly does not stop the round-start Natural Attack, so do not rely on stuns to shut it down.
The Natural Attack cheat can be dispelled (a Life dispel) to turn it off.
His Spirit Blast is a team-wide AoE dealing 1,120 Death, 1,095 Life, or 1,190 Myth damage.
His Spectral Blast is a team-wide AoE dealing 1,320 Fire, 1,095 Ice, or 1,650 Storm damage.
At 75% health (11,280 remaining) he interrupt-casts Spirit Blast, so blowing past this threshold in one hit avoids it.
At 50% health (7,520 remaining) he interrupt-casts Spirit Blast again.
The 50% trigger may fire the Spectral Blast instead of Spirit Blast.
At 25% health (3,760 remaining) he interrupt-casts another Spirit Blast.
Crossing several thresholds in one hit spreads the Blasts out over the following rounds rather than all at once, so a clean one-shot avoids them entirely.
